title: Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG Policy Analysis (Broadcom)
dataSources: Symantec WSS access logs (API-based collection via the Add-on)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="symantec:wss:accesslog" earliest=-7d
| where sc_filter_result="DENIED"
| stats count by cs_categories, cs_userdn, sc_filter_result
| sort -count
description: Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG (formerly WSS) provides cloud-delivered web security with URL filtering, threat protection, and SSL inspection for all users. Tracking policy violations and threat blocks validates security posture.
value: Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG (formerly WSS) provides cloud-delivered web security with URL filtering, threat protection, and SSL inspection for all users. Tracking policy violations and threat blocks validates security posture.
implementation: Install the Symantec WSS Add-on and configure API-based data collection. Map `cs_categories` to your acceptable-use policy. Alert when denied requests spike for a user. Report on top blocked categories and threat detections.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top users blocked), Line chart (daily denied trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Symantec WSS Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3856).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Symantec WSS access logs (API-based collection via the Add-on).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install the Symantec WSS Add-on and configure API-based data collection. Map `cs_categories` to your acceptable-use policy. Alert when denied requests spike for a user. Report on top blocked categories and threat detections.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="symantec:wss:accesslog" earliest=-7d
| where sc_filter_result="DENIED"
| stats count by cs_categories, cs_userdn, sc_filter_result
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG Policy Analysis (Broadcom)** — Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG (formerly WSS) provides cloud-delivered web security with URL filtering, threat protection, and SSL inspection for all users. Tracking policy violations and threat blocks validates security posture.

Documented **Data sources**: Symantec WSS access logs (API-based collection via the Add-on). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Symantec WSS Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3856). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: symantec:wss:accesslog. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype="symantec:wss:accesslog", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where sc_filter_result="DENIED"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cs_categories, cs_userdn, sc_filter_result** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Symantec/Broadcom cloud console for the same policies, incidents, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top users blocked), Line chart (daily denied trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Symantec Cloud SWG (cloud), Symantec Web Security Service Edge, Symantec Endpoint Agent
